title: Methods of communication
summary: 9609 A Level — verbal, written, visual, and electronic methods with suitability for context.
learningObjectives: Describe verbal, written, visual, and electronic methods.
Compare advantages and disadvantages of each.
Select appropriate methods for given scenarios.
Evaluate method choice in case organisations.

simpleExplanation.title: How the message is delivered
simpleExplanation.summary: Verbal communication is fast with immediate feedback; written creates a permanent record; visual aids clarify data; electronic methods span distance instantly — choose based on urgency, audience, and complexity.
steps: Verbal: meetings, phone — fast, rich feedback.
Written: emails, reports — record and precision.
Visual: charts, diagrams — simplify complex data.
Electronic: video calls, intranet — speed across sites.

flashcards.back: Fast, nuanced; no permanent record, easily misunderstood.
Clear record, can be detailed; slow, no immediate feedback.
Quick to grasp trends; can oversimplify or mislead.
Instant global reach; tech failure, informal tone risks.
Face-to-face verbal first — not public email.
Written report + visual charts in presentation.
Video + shared documents; supplement with periodic face-to-face.

sections.content: **Methods of communication** (7.2.2) — examiners want **justified choice**, not a list. Match **method to message**: redundancy news ≠ Slack emoji.

items: **Verbal:** Team briefings, appraisals — emotional and urgent messages.
**Written:** Policies, contracts, audit trails — formal and legal.
**Electronic:** Email, Teams, ERP alerts — daily coordination.

sections.question: HQ must communicate a 10% headcount reduction to three country offices. Recommend communication methods.
sections.solution:
**Phase 1 — Senior managers:** **Live video call** (electronic + verbal) — allow **Q&A**, show **empathy**; written **talking points** follow.

**Phase 2 — Affected staff:** **Face-to-face** (or video if remote) **individual meetings** with HR — **written confirmation** of terms.

**Phase 3 — Remaining staff:** **Town hall** + **written FAQ** on intranet — reduce **grapevine** rumour (7.2.3).

**Avoid:** Mass **email only** — cold, leaks early, damages **trust**.
